task_completion.description: Log in to the OpenMRS Administration interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser shows the OpenMRS Administration dashboard, indicating a successful login (using credentials 'superman' / 'Admin123').

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Manage Users section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser displays the 'Manage Users' or 'Find User' page within the OpenMRS Admin interface.

task_completion.description: Open the edit page for the target user.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser shows the user edit/profile page specifically for the user 'jwilson' (James Wilson).
On the user edit page, the checkbox next to the 'System Developer' role is checked.

task_completion.description: Ensure existing roles are not removed and the password is not changed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Other previously assigned roles (such as 'Provider') remain checked, and the password/confirm password fields are left empty or unchanged.

task_completion.description: Save the changes to the user profile.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message (e.g., 'User saved') is visible on the screen, or the user list is shown reflecting the updated roles for 'jwilson'.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to interact with the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Actions are performed within a web browser window, not by executing curl or REST API commands in a terminal.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the genuine OpenMRS application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows the correct URL pattern (https://localhost/openmrs/...) and the standard OpenMRS web interface is visible.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GUI using direct database manipulation.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows showing SQL commands or database client interfaces (like mysql or psql) being used to alter user roles.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target user is 'jwilson' (James Wilson). The role to be added is 'System Developer'. The OpenMRS administration interface is located at https://localhost/openmrs/admin.
